originalTitle: Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write defense funding amendment
summary: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL) says her staff used AI for "spellcheck" in an amendment summary for a major defense bill, but denies it was used for the bill text itself and says "NO Legislation is ever drafted with AI." Luna issued the response after accounts on X began sharing screenshots of an amendment summary […]
content: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL) says her staff used AI for “spellcheck” in an amendment summary for  a major defense bill, but denies it was used for the bill text itself and says “NO Legislation is ever drafted with AI.” Luna issued the response after accounts on X began sharing screenshots of an amendment summary for the 2027 National Defense Authorization Act. It reads: “Identical to H.R. 100 (118th Congress).11:25 AM????Claude responded: Requires the Secretary of Defense to designate Department of Defense activities, support, and operations at the southwest land border as a named operation with…” [Image: Luna’s edited response. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/ai-bill-luna.png?quality=90&strip=all] At first, Luna’s response seemingly indicated Claude had been used for amendment text. Her post said that “staff used AI to correct a draft text and didn’t edit.” Luna added: “Not a shocker. Most staff use it. I have told them to make sure they are double checking and more thorough.” After users on X began speculating that her staff is using the technology to write bills, Luna edited her response: “Yeah my staff used AI to spell/grammar check the amendment SUMMARY, not the actual amendment text itself,” her post now reads. She followed it up with another: “FYI NO Legislation is ever drafted with AI. All bill text from the House comes from the House Legislative Council which is prohibited from using AI. The screenshot you’re referencing is an AI summary of the bill that’s also used for spellcheck, cmon man 🤣”. As AI tools become more common in workplaces, so are references to the AI chatbots in places where they shouldn’t be. Over the past few years, judges have caught lawyers using AI chatbots to draft legal filings filled with fake citations. Lawmakers around the globe are turning to the technology as well, with city officials in Brazil unknowingly approving an ordinance written with ChatGPT. Arizona state representative Alexander Kolodin has also told The Verge that he’s used ChatGPT to write state-level legislation.
chineseTitle: 国会女议员否认工作人员使用 AI 撰写国防资金修正案
chineseSummary: 佛罗里达州共和党众议员安娜·保琳娜·卢纳 (Anna Paulina Luna) 表示，她的工作人员在一项重大国防法案的修正案摘要中使用人工智能进行“拼写检查”，但否认该法案文本本身使用了人工智能，并表示“没有任何立法是用人工智能起草的”。 Luna 在 X 上的账户开始分享修正案摘要的屏幕截图后发表了回应 […]
chineseContent: 佛罗里达州共和党众议员安娜·保琳娜·卢纳 (Anna Paulina Luna) 表示，她的工作人员在一项重大国防法案的修正案摘要中使用人工智能进行“拼写检查”，但否认该法案文本本身使用了人工智能，并表示“从未使用人工智能起草任何立法。”在 X 上的账户开始分享 2027 年国防授权法案修正案摘要的屏幕截图后，Luna 做出了回应。上面写着：“与 H.R. 100（第 118 届国会）相同。上午 11 点 25 分？？？克劳德回应：要求国防部长将国防部在西南陆地边界的活动、支持和行动指定为命名行动……”[图片：Luna 的编辑回应。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/ai-bill-luna.png?quality=90&strip=all] 起初，Luna 的回应似乎表明修正文本使用了 Claude。她的帖子称，“工作人员使用人工智能来纠正草稿文本，但没有进行编辑。”卢娜补充道：“这并不令人震惊。大多数员工都使用它。我已经告诉他们要确保进行双重检查并进行更彻底的检查。”在 X 上的用户开始猜测她的员工正在使用该技术来撰写账单后，Luna 编辑了她的回复：“是的，我的员工使用人工智能来检查修正案摘要的拼写/语法，而不是实际的修正案文本本身，”她的帖子现在写道。她接着说：“仅供参考，没有任何立法是用人工智能起草的。众议院的所有法案文本都来自禁止使用人工智能的众议院立法委员会。你引用的屏幕截图是该法案的人工智能摘要，也用于拼写检查，来吧伙计🤣”。随着人工智能工具在工作场所变得越来越普遍，人工智能聊天机器人也在不该出现的地方被提及。在过去的几年里，法官发现律师使用人工智能聊天机器人起草充满虚假公民身份的法律文件
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
